name: Numeric Truncation Error
description: When a primitive is cast to a smaller primitive, high-order bits are lost, potentially resulting in unexpected values. This data loss can corrupt critical values used for array indexing, size calculations, or state management, leading to integrity violations.
detection_pattern: Look for INGRESS or STORAGE nodes containing a numeric value flowing through a TRANSFORM node (narrowing cast to smaller type) without a CONTROL node (range/bounds validation) ensuring the value fits in the target type. The truncated result may cause incorrect indexing or logic errors downstream.
